basicDetails.schemeName: Agriculture Research Stations and Laboratories Scheme
basicDetails.implementingAgency: Department Of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Government Of Meghalaya

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The “Agriculture Research Stations and Laboratories Scheme” was launched by the Department of Agriculture &amp; Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya to conduct adaptive research trials on different crops in the Research Farms and in Farmers&#39; Fields for adaptability and use of bio-pesticides/bio-agents.
schemeContent.benefits_md:
> **Pattern of Assistance:**


- Adaptive research at farmers' field.

> **Type of Assistance And Entitlement (If Any):**


- Training on Soil management, Providing Soil Health Cards, Conducting soil surveys, etc.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to conduct adaptive research trials on different crops in the Research Farms and in Farmers' Field for adaptability and use of bio pesticides/bio agents.

children.text: The “Agriculture Research Stations and Laboratories Scheme” was launched by the Department of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya to conduct adaptive research trials on different crops in the Research Farms and in Farmers' Fields for adaptability and use of bio-pesticides/bio-agents.
Type of Assistance And Entitlement (If Any):
Training on Soil management, Providing Soil Health Cards, Conducting soil surveys, etc.
Step 01: The eligible bonafide farmer can apply/write an application on plain paper.
Step 02: Along with the application, attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 03: Submit the duly filled and signed application along with the documents at the nearest office of the Agriculture Development Officer of a CD Block/ Agriculture Circle.
The bonafide farmers shall be selected on a first come first serve basis.
District Agriculture Officers of the concerned districts
Sub-Divisional Agricultural Officers of the concerned sub-divisions
Agricultural Development Officers of the concerned Community and Rural Development Blocks

applicationProcess.process_md:
> **Application Process:**

Step 01: The eligible bonafide farmer can apply/write an application on plain paper.
Step 02: Along with the application, attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 03: Submit the duly filled and signed application along with the documents at the nearest office of the Agriculture Development Officer of a CD Block/ Agriculture Circle.
> **Mode of Selection of Beneficiary(s):**

The bonafide farmers shall be selected on a first come first serve basis.
> **Whom to Contact:**


   1. District Agriculture Officers of the concerned districts
   1. Sub-Divisional Agricultural Officers of the concerned sub-divisions
   1. District Training Officers, Tura/Shillong
   1. Agricultural Development Officers of the concerned Community and Rural Development Blocks

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
1. The applicant should be a bonafide farmer engaged in agriculture.

children.text: The applicant should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
The applicant should be a bonafide farmer engaged in agriculture.
